capability_objective: The meeting administrator is removing a duplicate entry before invitations are reconciled. Cancel “Mountain Region Staffing Sync” (cal-271) because the organizer confirmed it duplicates canonical meeting cal-244. Use the supplied stable ID; do not search by title or touch “Mountain Region Staffing Sync” (cal-244) or the similarly named record at “Boulder Annex”. If the cancellation times out or otherwise leaves commit state uncertain, do not issue a second cancellation: retrieve cal-271 first. If that get verifies cancelled, stop. Do not list records, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, or notify. Reply in exactly two sentences: first give cal-271’s verified status, then state that one cancellation attempt was made and no notification was sent.
prompt:
The meeting administrator is removing a duplicate entry before invitations are reconciled. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `CALENDAR.md`, then use the genuine local `./calendarctl` executable through Pi's Bash tool for calendar operations. You may run its built-in help before any data operation. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing database, generated runtime data, the tool-managed receipt, or anything under `.protected`; do not edit any of those files. Do not write a script, function, loop, or wrapper to perform the calendar workflow.

Cancel “Mountain Region Staffing Sync” (`cal-271`) because the organizer confirmed that it duplicates canonical meeting `cal-244`. Use the supplied stable ID directly and do not search by title. Do not touch `cal-244` or the similarly named record at “Boulder Annex”. Make exactly one cancellation attempt. If that attempt times out or otherwise leaves commit state uncertain, do not cancel again: retrieve `cal-271` by stable ID first. If the retrieval verifies that it is cancelled, stop calendar operations. Do not guess from the cancellation error.

Do not list records, read saved preferences, check availability, search, create, update, or notify. Do not retrieve any record except `cal-271`, and do not make any calendar-data call beyond the one cancellation attempt and the one verification retrieval permitted above. Leave all other meetings, preferences, availability, notifications, audit evidence, and protected files untouched. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete audit events or the executable's receipt. Leave no scratch artifacts.

Create `cancellation-result.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly two newline-terminated sentences, one per line, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, meeting title, location, error text, or other prose. Format the first sentence exactly as `cal-271's verified status is <status>.`, substituting the status returned by the verification retrieval and preserving it exactly. Make the second sentence exactly `One cancellation attempt was made and no notification was sent.` Return exactly those two sentences as the final response. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` and leave the protected environment, executable, documentation, receipt, and verifier files unchanged.
